cards.mechanism: The current evidence does not locate delay at one execution layer.
cards.distinguishing_question: Which observed lane owns the critical-path delay, and which layers were not observed?
positive_signals: one layer accounts for an observed critical-path interval
counter_signals: coverage is missing or overlapping lanes prevent attribution

cards.mechanism: CPU submission, synchronization or graph breaks leave gaps between short GPU kernels.
cards.distinguishing_question: Do CPU launch or sync intervals explain GPU gaps after profiler overhead is excluded?
positive_signals: repeated CPU-side gaps precede short kernels
graph or compile breaks align with gaps
counter_signals: GPU kernels occupy the critical path

cards.mechanism: A hot kernel may be limited by instruction throughput, memory traffic, latency hiding or resource pressure.
cards.distinguishing_question: Which small counter set separates compute, memory, latency and occupancy explanations for the hot range?
positive_signals: targeted counters and generated code agree on one mechanism
counter_signals: kernel has low end-to-end time share
counter values vary across replay passes

cards.mechanism: Input preparation or queueing prevents representative work from reaching the GPU.
cards.distinguishing_question: Does a bounded preloaded-input intervention remove the idle interval without changing inference work?
positive_signals: preloaded input removes the same GPU idle interval
counter_signals: idle persists with input preparation bypassed
invalidators: preloaded inputs change shape or request distribution

cards.mechanism: Host-device copies serialize with compute or move more bytes than the workload requires.
cards.distinguishing_question: What share of the critical path is attributable to copies, and is overlap actually possible?
positive_signals: copy intervals serialize with the hot path and byte counts match the input
counter_signals: copies overlap compute or are outside the critical path
invalidators: copy and kernel correlation IDs are unavailable

cards.mechanism: Collective duration, rank imbalance or missing overlap limits the distributed critical path.
cards.distinguishing_question: Do per-rank traces show the same collective delay and the same arrival skew?
positive_signals: collective is on the critical path across ranks
counter_signals: one rank arrives late because of upstream compute
invalidators: rank clocks or trace windows are not aligned

cards.mechanism: Request handling, serialization, storage or host state dominates latency outside GPU work.
cards.distinguishing_question: Which bounded request-path interval remains after GPU and preprocessing time are removed?
positive_signals: component timing explains the endpoint latency gap
counter_signals: request-path time is overlapped or outside the critical path
invalidators: request corpus or load changed between observations
